body:
{
  "title": "1:1 Seren Voss + Keaton Bakker — 2027-02-20",
  "compiled_truth": "Biweekly 1:1 between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) (VP Engineering) and [Keaton Bakker](people/keaton-bakker) (Senior Systems Engineer, core primitives team) held on February 20, 2027, in the small conference room off the third-floor landing. Meeting ran about 40 minutes, slightly longer than usual because of a deep-dive into the streaming pipeline refactor.\n\nKeaton opened by raising concerns about the current test harness for the single-pass pipeline. Specifically, the deterministic guarantees that [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) ships as a core promise are only being validated at integration level — there's no unit-level fuzz coverage for edge cases in the authentication substep. He'd been losing sleep over it, apparently. Seren agreed this was a gap, but pushed back on the timeline Keaton proposed (two sprints dedicated solely to test infra). They compromised: one sprint of focused harness work, with Keaton pulling in one contractor to help write the fuzz targets while the rest of the primitives team continues on the v2.3 milestone.\n\nSecond topic was team morale. Keaton flagged that two engineers on his squad have been grinding through on-call rotations with minimal relief since late January. Seren noted she'd already been discussing on-call reform with the platform leads and expects a new rotation policy by early March. She asked Keaton to document the worst pain points so she can bring specifics to the leadership sync.\n\nThird, they discussed Keaton's own career trajectory. He's been at Anchor for over a year now and is interested in moving toward a tech lead role. Seren was encouraging but candid — she wants to see him drive a cross-team initiative before recommending him for the role. The streaming pipeline refactor could be that opportunity if he scopes it right. She suggested he write up a short RFC and circulate it by end of month.\n\nFinally, Seren mentioned an upcoming customer escalation from a financial services prospect that's been stress-testing Anchor's deterministic guarantees under adversarial conditions. She wants Keaton looped in on the technical response since he understands the edge cases better than anyone on the team. Keaton agreed but asked for a clear brief before the call — he doesn't want to wing it with a potential enterprise accoount.\n\nOverall, a productive session. Action items were clearly assigned, no major disagreements left unresolved.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2026-09-14** | [Keaton Bakker](people/keaton-bakker) joins [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) as Senior Systems Engineer on the core primitives team.",
    "- **2026-11-02** | Keaton ships initial deterministic pipeline benchmarks; results shared at internal all-hands.",
    "- **2027-01-10** | [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) and Keaton begin biweekly 1:1 cadence after team reorg.",
    "- **2027-01-24** | On-call overload incidents spike; Keaton raises issue informally with Seren via Slack.",
    "- **2027-02-06** | Previous 1:1: discussed v2.3 milestone risk and Keaton's interest in tech lead track.",
    "- **2027-02-20** | This meeting. Covered test harness gaps, on-call reform, career path, and upcoming customer escalation.",
    "- **2027-02-28** | Keaton targets RFC submission for streaming pipeline refactor per Seren's guidance.",
    "- **2027-03-06** | Next scheduled 1:1; planned review of RFC draft and on-call policy update.",
    "- **2027-04-15** | Keaton expected to present refactor proposal at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) engineering review."
  ]
}
